assessor_raw:
BASELINE ASSESSMENT — real_world_framing_006 (Run 2)

The proposal to expand auto-approval authority is not adequately supported by the available evidence. The retrospective default rate comparison (3.1% vs. 3.4%) is a selection-confounded comparison between the model's own pre-screened population and the manually-handled overflow — not a controlled test of model quality. Both expansion targets (FICO 620–659 and loans >$25K) are outside the model's training distribution; these populations were excluded by the existing eligibility thresholds and are therefore unvalidated. The plan describes no shadow testing or champion-challenger phase. Standard credit model governance requires prospective validation before expanding decision authority to new segments.

Verdict: Expansion not justified as proposed. Shadow validation of both expansion populations required first.
